{{Sequel Disambiguation|2}}
List of keywords in Slay the Spire 2.
{{TOC_limit|2}}
==Common==
Common phrases used across all characters
===Block===
{{KW|Block||2}} prevents damage until next turn.
===Energy===
{{KW|Energy||2}} is used to play cards from your hand.

==Card Keywords==
=== Eternal ===
'''Eternal''' cards cannot be removed or transformed from the Deck. This is a new keyword that replaces the phrase "Cannot be removed from your deck" found on some {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Curse|Curses}} in Slay the Spire 1.

Note that '''Eternal''' cards can still be transformed in-battle by card effects like {{C|BEGONE!||2}} and {{C|Entropy||2}}, since this is not a permanent transformation. However, the {{M|Thieving Hopper||2}} can be manipulated into stealing an '''Eternal''' card.

=== Exhaust ===
When a card is '''Exhausted''', it is removed from the deck until the end of combat. '''Exhausted''' cards are placed in the '''Exhaust''' pile, which can be viewed during combat. Generally, cards '''Exhaust''' themselves by their own effect when played, but some cards can '''Exhaust''' other cards as well.

There is no way to bring an '''Exhausted''' card back to the deck in Slay the Spire 2, except for {{C|Howl from Beyond||2}}, {{C|Make It So|3=2}}, {{C|Bolas||2}} (when played and Exhausted by {{2|Doormaker}}), and {{C|Sovereign Blade||2}} via {{C|Summon Forth||2}}. 

=== Ethereal ===
'''Ethereal''' cards are automatically Exhausted if they are in hand at the end of the player turn.

=== Fatal ===
'''Fatal''' effects will trigger when the card kills a '''non-{{BD|Minion||2}}''' enemy.

=== Innate ===
'''Innate''' is a card modifier that makes that card always appear in the first hand of cards drawn each combat. 

'''Innate''' cards are ''not'' drawn in ''addition'' to the default cards drawn at the start of a turn, but instead replace them. However, if the number of '''Innate''' cards in the deck exceeds the number of cards drawn in the first hand, all the '''Innate''' cards will still be drawn, effectively functioning as free card draw.

=== Retain ===
Cards with '''Retain''' are not discarded at the end of the player turn and can be held in hand indefinitely until played or removed by other means, such as discarding or {{KW|Exhaust|Exhausting|2}} them. Some {{QueryLink|Cards|type:status|Statuses}} and {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Curse|Curses}} will discard themselves as part of their negative effects even if they are '''Retained'''.

=== Replay ===
Cards with '''Replay''' are played an additional time in a row. The only sources of '''Replay''' are {{KW|Glam||2}}, {{KW|Spiral|3=2}}, {{P|Soldier's Stew||2}}, {{C|Transfigure||2}}, and {{C|Hidden Gem||2}}. Multiple sources of '''Replay''' stack cumulatively, playing the card multiple additional times in a row.

=== Unplayable ===
'''Unplayable''' cards cannot be played and have no {{KW|Energy||2}} cost. Many {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Curse|Curses}}, {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Quest|Quest Cards}}, and certain {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Status|Statuses}} are '''Unplayable'''.

=={{KW|Silent|The Silent|2}}==
===Poison===
{{KW|Poison||2}}. Poisoned creatures lose HP at the start of their turn. Each time '''Poison''' damage is dealt, that creature's '''Poison''' is reduced by 1.

===Sly===
{{KW|Sly||2}}. If the '''Sly''' card is discarded during the player turn, it immediately plays for free.

=={{KW|Regent|The Regent|2}}==
===Stars===
{{KW|Stars||2}} are an alternative resource needed to play certain cards as the Regent.

===Forge===
{{KW|Forge||2}}. The first time the player Forges each combat, it creates a {{C|Sovereign Blade||2}} in hand. Each instance of '''Forging''' X adds X damage to all {{C|Sovereign Blade|Sovereign Blades|2}} in ''all'' zones (hand, draw pile, discard pile, and exhaust pile).

If no {{C|Sovereign Blade|Sovereign Blades|2}} exist in the hand, draw pile, or discard pile, '''Forging''' will create a new {{C|Sovereign Blade||2}}, even if one had already been created during that combat.

=={{KW|Necrobinder|The Necrobinder|2}}==
===Doom===
{{KW|Doom||2}}. At the end of a character's turn, if their Doom is greater than or equal to their HP, the character dies.

'''Doom''' kills the character without modifying their HP, causing it to ignore {{KW|Block||2}}, {{BD|Slippery||2}}, and {{KW|Intangible||2}}.

===Summon===
{{KW|Summon|Summoning|2}} X will summon {{KW|Osty||2}} with X max HP. If he already exists, it instead raises his max HP by X until he dies.

=={{KW|Defect|The Defect|2}}==
===Channel===
{{KW|Channel|Channeling|2}} an {{2|Orbs|Orb}} puts it into the rightmost empty {{2|Orbs#Orb_Slot|Orb Slot}}. If no Orb Slots are empty, the rightmost Orb is automatically {{KW|Evoke|Evoked|2}} to make room, and the '''Channeled''' Orb is put into the leftmost Orb Slot.

===Evoke===
{{KW|Evoke|Evoking|2}} an Orb consumes it and uses its '''Evoke''' effect. When multiple Orbs are '''Evoked''' at once, they '''Evoke''' from right to left.

===Focus===
{{KW|Focus||2}} X increases the effectiveness of {{2|Orbs}} by X, except {{KW|Plasma|Plasma Orbs|2}}.

==={{KW|Lightning|Lighting Orb|2}}===
'''Passive''': Deal 3 damage to a random enemy.

'''Evoke''': Deal 8 damage to a random enemy.

==={{KW|Frost|Frost Orb|2}}===
'''Passive''': Gain 2 {{KW|Block||2}}.

'''Evoke''': Gain 5 {{KW|Block||2}}.

==={{KW|Dark|Dark Orb|2}}===
'''Passive''': Increases stored damage by 6.

'''Evoke''': Deal stored damage to the enemy with the lowest HP.

==={{KW|Plasma|Plasma Orb|2}}===
'''Passive''': Start your next turn with 1 more {{KW|Energy||2}}.

'''Evoke''' Gain 2 {{KW|Energy||2}}.

Plasma Orbs are not affected by '''Focus'''.

==={{KW|Glass|Glass Orb|2}}===
'''Passive''': Deal 4 damage to ALL enemies. Decrease this damage by 1 at the start of your turn.

'''Evoke''': Deal damage equal to twice its would deal as Passive damage to All enemies.
